Description: PHP dependency manager. Installs packages from Packagist. Manages autoloading and scripts
Command:
composer require vendor/package
composer install --no-dev --optimize-autoloader
composer update
composer dump-autoload -o
When To Use: Always — every PHP project uses Composer for dependency management
Output: Installs packages to vendor/. Generates autoload files. Updates composer.lock

Description: Static analysis tool that finds bugs without running code. 10 strictness levels (0-9)
When To Use: On every commit and in CI. Use level 5-6 for existing projects; 8-9 for new projects
Output: Reports type errors unused code unreachable statements and potential bugs

Description: Another static analysis tool with taint analysis for security vulnerability detection
When To Use: When security analysis is critical. Can complement or replace PHPStan
Output: Finds type errors taint analysis detects security vulnerabilities in data flow

Description: Automatically fixes PHP code to follow coding standards (PSR-12 and custom rules)
When To Use: Before every commit. Run in CI to enforce consistent style
Output: Reformats code to match configured rules. Shows diff of changes

Description: Opinionated PHP code formatter built on top of PHP CS Fixer with Laravel defaults
When To Use: For Laravel projects — zero-config code formatting
Output: Formats code with sensible Laravel-friendly defaults

Description: Automated refactoring and PHP version upgrade tool. Transforms code using rules
When To Use: When upgrading PHP versions. When refactoring patterns at scale (e.g. adding type hints)
Output: Applies code transformations: adds types modernizes syntax upgrades deprecated code

Description: PHP extension for debugging with breakpoints step-through and profiling
When To Use: For complex debugging. For profiling performance bottlenecks. IDE integration with PHPStorm/VSCode
Output: Step-through debugging with variable inspection. Generates cachegrind profiles

Description: The standard PHP testing framework. Unit and integration testing with assertions and mocks
When To Use: For all PHP testing. Run on every commit and in CI
Output: Test results with pass/fail counts. Coverage reports in text/HTML/Clover format

Description: Expressive testing framework built on top of PHPUnit with a cleaner syntax
When To Use: For teams preferring BDD-style syntax. Drop-in PHPUnit replacement
Output: Same output as PHPUnit with cleaner test syntax and better error messages

Description: Laravel's built-in CLI tool for migrations code generation queue management and more
Command:
php artisan make:model User -mfc
php artisan migrate
php artisan tinker
php artisan make:controller UserController --api
php artisan test
php artisan queue:work
php artisan schedule:run
When To Use: For all Laravel development tasks: scaffolding database management testing queues
Output: Generates boilerplate code. Runs migrations. Interactive REPL. Queue processing

Description: Standalone CLI component for building command-line applications
Command:
php bin/console list
php bin/console make:entity
php bin/console doctrine:migrations:migrate
php bin/console cache:clear
When To Use: For Symfony projects and any PHP CLI application
Output: Command output with styling tables and progress bars

Description: Development toolbar showing queries timing views and more in the browser
Command: composer require --dev barryvdh/laravel-debugbar\n# Auto-enabled when APP_DEBUG=true\n# Shows in browser at bottom of page
When To Use: During development to identify N+1 queries slow queries and memory usage
Output: Browser toolbar with query count timing memory usage view rendering details

Description: Debug assistant that records requests exceptions queries jobs mail and more
Command:
composer require laravel/telescope --dev
php artisan telescope:install
php artisan migrate
# Visit /telescope
When To Use: For development and staging debugging. Inspect failed jobs slow queries exceptions
Output: Web dashboard at /telescope showing all application activity

Description: Detects code smells: too complex methods excessive coupling unused parameters
When To Use: In CI pipeline to catch code quality issues. Complements PHPStan
Output: Reports code smells with rule references and suggestions

Description: Tokenizes PHP code to detect violations of coding standards and can auto-fix some
Command:
./vendor/bin/phpcs --standard=PSR12 src/
./vendor/bin/phpcbf --standard=PSR12 src/
# phpcs = detect
# phpcbf = auto-fix
When To Use: When PHP CS Fixer or Pint is not used. Good for enforcing custom sniffs
Output: Lists coding standard violations with file line and rule reference

Description: Dashboard and configuration for Laravel Redis queues with real-time monitoring
Command:
composer require laravel/horizon
php artisan horizon:install
php artisan horizon
# Visit /horizon
When To Use: For production Redis queue management. Monitor jobs throughput failures
Output: Web dashboard at /horizon with real-time job throughput failure rates and metrics

Description: Define custom scripts in composer.json for common development tasks
When To Use: \n    \"lint\": \"./vendor/bin/phpstan analyse\"
Output: \n    \"format\": \"./vendor/bin/pint\"
column11: To standardize common commands across the team. For CI/CD pipelines
column12: Runs defined commands. Can chain multiple scripts together

Description: Built-in PHP-FPM status page for monitoring worker processes and request statistics
When To Use: For production monitoring. Track active processes listen queue and slow requests
Output: Process count active/idle workers listen queue length slow request log

Description: Interactive PHP REPL with your full application context loaded
Command:
php artisan tinker
>>> User::count()
>>> User::where('name', 'like', '%john%')->get()
>>> event(new App\Events\UserRegistered(User::first()))
>>> app()->make(App\Services\OrderService::class)
When To Use: For quick data exploration debugging and testing code snippets with your app context
Output: Full application context with autoloaded models services and helpers

Description: Containerize PHP application with PHP-FPM and Nginx for consistent development and deployment
When To Use: For all environments from development to production. Ensures consistency across team
Output: Running PHP-FPM + Nginx + MySQL + Redis containers
